cards.name: Los Gatos Cafe Uptown
cards.address: 15662 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at this Los Gatos cafe with a proper breakfast and good coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: Solid local spot that closes at 2 PM, so morning is the move.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the galleries and take your time in the sculpture garden — it's free, uncrowded, and genuinely impressive for a local museum.
cards.why: Free contemporary art with a sculpture garden is a perfect way to spend a slow Sunday morning.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out this optical illusion and perception-based exhibition — the kind of show that's actually fun to walk through, not just stare at.
cards.why: It's free, it's in Santa Clara, and it pairs perfectly with the Triton visit for an art-heavy afternoon.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Pick a vendor or three and graze your way through lunch at San Pedro Square — tacos, banh mi, ramen, coffee, whatever sounds good.
cards.why: Best all-in-one food stop in the area and a great spot to settle in and decompress for a bit.

cards.name: Slingshot Pinball
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #70, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a machine at Slingshot and play pinball for a couple hours — they've got a great selection and it's genuinely fun for adults.
cards.why: Beats sitting around and it's one of the better ways to spend a rainy Sunday evening.

cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a donut and coffee from this highly loved vegan cafe on Santa Clara St — the donuts are the real draw, so grab a couple and take your time.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hit it early before the good stuff sells out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition on your way through Santa Clara — it's a visually interesting show worth at least an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, and daytime-only so now is the time to catch it.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and just pick whatever looks good — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof and you can grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown with no wrong answer on what to order.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre for the SJSU Opera Theatre double bill — La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator are two very different pieces and together they make for a genuinely interesting afternoon of live performance.
cards.why: It's free and it's live opera in a great venue, which is a rare Sunday afternoon treat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge, where you pick a piece, paint it however you want, and they fire it for you — genuinely fun for adults and a great low-key way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Wander through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of visual art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely interesting way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history and culture of martial arts in America.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and the kind of thing you'd never stumble on otherwise.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, ramen, banh mi, whatever sounds good. Snag a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch downtown when you can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at the SJSU Student Union Bowling Center. It's a real lanes setup, not a pop-up, and it's open to the public.
cards.why: Low-key competitive and way more fun than it sounds on a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row in the early evening, browse the boutiques, and find a table at one of the outdoor restaurants when you're ready to sit down.
cards.why: The boulevard is relaxed on a weekday evening and the patio vibes are solid.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - San Jose
cards.address: 1495 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at LUNA Mexican Kitchen on The Alameda. Order margaritas and something off the dinner menu and call it a good Monday.
cards.why: Solid Mexican food with a vibe that doesn't feel rushed.

cards.name: The Midwife and The Baker
cards.address: 846 Independence Ave, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Midwife and the Baker is a Mountain View morning institution — grab a pastry and coffee and ease into the day.
cards.why: Hard to beat a bakery this good on a cool morning.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Rancho San Antonio — the loop out to the farm overlook is a solid two hours without being brutal.
cards.why: Cool temps make this the perfect window for a hike before it gets busy.

cards.name: Volunteering at Stanford Educational Farm - Weekday
cards.address: 175 Electioneer Rd, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours volunteering at Stanford's Educational Farm, working in the fields alongside others on a weekday morning session.
cards.why: It's a genuinely satisfying way to spend time outside and give back.

cards.name: Katsu
cards.address: 859 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Katsu in Mountain View has an absurdly good reputation for a reason — try the local favorite and don't skip the sides.
cards.why: You'll want a real lunch after a morning of physical activity.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Erika Chong Shuch's exhibition at Stanford is an immersive new work worth exploring slowly — give yourself time to sit with it.
cards.why: Free contemporary art at Stanford is always worth the stop.

cards.name: Arisa Thai Massage
cards.address: 4546 El Camino Real Suite 203, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Arisa Thai Massage in Los Altos and let someone sort out whatever the hike did to your legs.
cards.why: After a full morning outside, this is exactly what the afternoon calls for.

cards.name: Laasya Art Gallery
cards.address: 2225 E Bayshore Rd #100-7127, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening browsing Laasya Art Gallery in Palo Alto, which carries eclectic work worth more than a quick glance.
cards.why: A relaxed gallery visit is a perfect low-key cap to an active day.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning in Japantown — grab mochi at Okayama Kobo and a bowl of udon at Gombei when they open, then wander Jackson St and browse the Japanese grocery.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and a genuinely great way to ease into a Friday.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Real mummies, actual hieroglyphics, and an Egyptian temple tucked into a San Jose neighborhood. Wander the galleries and take your time in the planetarium courtyard.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the most surprising places in the South Bay and nobody expects it to be this good.

cards.name: Little Wine House
cards.address: 350 W Julian St Bldg 4, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Little Wine House opens at 4 PM and it's the right call for a pre-dinner drink. Settle in, try a glass, and let the evening start slowly.
cards.why: A perfect gear-shift between afternoon and dinner without rushing anywhere.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Free contemporary art and a sculpture garden you can walk through at your own pace. The Triton punches well above its weight for a free museum.
cards.why: Free, uncrowded, and the sculpture garden is perfect on a clear afternoon.

cards.name: Elyse Restaurant
cards.address: 96 S 1st St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Elyse Restaurant opens at 5 PM so you can snag an early table before the Friday crowd fills it up. Order whatever looks good and take your time.
cards.why: It's a Friday night and you deserve a proper sit-down dinner.

cards.name: $9 Before 9 pm Happy Hour
cards.address: 48 South 1st Street, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to 48 South 1st Street for the $9 Before 9 PM Happy Hour. It's a great low-key cap to the night with a drink in hand.
cards.why: Nine bucks for a happy hour drink downtown on a Friday is a solid deal.

cards.address: 14665 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Sue's Gallery Cafe on Big Basin Way. Grab a coffee and something from the menu on the patio and ease into the day.
cards.why: It's a charming spot to kick off a Friday morning before the day gets going.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Fremont Older Open Space. Take the upper loop for the sweeping foothill views — it's genuinely beautiful and you'll probably have the trails mostly to yourself.
cards.why: 73°F high and morning cool makes this the perfect window for a hike.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. Walk the sculpture garden, then spend time with the contemporary galleries inside. It's free and genuinely world-class for a local institution.
cards.why: You'll be glad you went — most people sleep on this place.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Check out Jessica Monette's Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming in the Project Room at SCU. It's a contemplative, intimate exhibition worth slowing down for.
cards.why: Free art, good vibes, and it's already in your orbit for the afternoon.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon at Picchetti Winery up on Montebello Road. Grab a glass of wine, sit outside, and take in the hillside views. It closes at 5 so get there with enough time to settle in.
cards.why: A gorgeous way to end the active part of the day before dinner.

cards.name: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House | San Jose
cards.address: 1410 El Paseo de Saratoga, San Jose, CA 95130, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House in San Jose. Go for the sukiyaki hot pot and let the evening slow down over a proper sit-down meal.
cards.why: It's the kind of dinner that feels like an event on its own.

cards.address: 2165 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Iraklis for lunch — solid Greek food on El Camino, the kind of place where you order more than you planned to and don't regret it.
cards.why: Under $15 a head for food this good is a deal you take.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: While you're already there, walk through the State of Illusion exhibition and see how the two shows play off each other — very different vibes in the same building.
cards.why: Two shows, one stop, zero dollars.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Triton Museum of Art, including time in the sculpture garden out back where the afternoon light actually makes everything look better.
cards.why: It's consistently one of the best free art spaces in the South Bay.

cards.name: Knit-Alongs at Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: Swing by the Knit-Along at Cupertino Library — bring your own project or just show up and see what people are working on, it's a low-key, no-pressure hang.
cards.why: If you knit or have been meaning to get back into it, this is a surprisingly fun Friday afternoon thing.

cards.name: Moods. Wine Bar and Bistro
cards.address: 111 W Evelyn Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Moods Wine Bar and Bistro on Evelyn Ave. Order a glass of something good, share some small plates, and settle in.
cards.why: A 4.9 you don't need to dress up for is a rare thing, and Friday night here just feels right.

cards.name: Izzy's Brooklyn Bagels
cards.address: 477 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a bagel at Izzy's — grab a classic with lox and cream cheese and eat it at one of the outdoor tables on California Ave.
cards.why: Best bagels in Palo Alto, full stop.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit the Stanford Foothills trail up to the Dish for sweeping views of the Bay and the peninsula. The climb is worth it and the wide fire roads make for a great brisk walk.

cards.why: Poolside patio on a warm spring Friday is a very good idea.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA

cards.address: 170 State St Unit 121, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA

cards.why: It's a great midday reset after a busy morning.

cards.address: 334 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Gourmet Works on Main St in Los Altos. Browse the cooking gear, pick up something fun, or just wander through one of the better kitchen shops in the area.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun browse even if you're not planning to cook anything soon.

cards.name: K Spa and Bodywork
cards.address: 151 2nd St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage or bodywork session at K Spa in Los Altos. It's a proper spa, so plan ahead if you can, but walk-ins sometimes work on a Friday afternoon.
cards.why: Best way to wind down before dinner.

cards.why: Light, sweet, and the perfect low-key way to end a Friday.
